summary: Migrated the default prototype map fixture to an explicit COMPOSITE topology/route graph with 14 districts, 25 topology route edges, variable route degree, route-node endpoints, river/coast/road modes, an explicit no-known-route isolation, and compiler/runtime/sim-core boot coverage so authority no longer derives from rectangular, hex, sequential-id, or hidden-lattice adjacency.

decisions.decision: Inherited the prior writer's allowed-path partial diff without reverting it, reviewed the full diff, and made only targeted fixes for concrete issues found during validation.
Default player-facing prototype content now uses explicit topology.routeEdges and routeNodes as the authoritative movement/adjacency surface; the runtime manifest hash includes topology.
The fixture remains synthetic COMPOSITE validation content and does not assert exact historical borders, ports, river importance, or route importance.

Adjusted dry-season river route multipliers on route-004, route-009, and route-020 so route preview demonstrates explicit seasonal road-vs-river path choice without deriving movement from geometry or district id order.

risks.summary: District names/geometry/routes are low-fidelity COMPOSITE placeholders and need historical_researcher confirmation that no source-backed historical claims are implied.
Client rendering/hit testing is outside this writer scope and must consume the new topology-backed read models in the downstream client task.

requested_action: Review the COMPOSITE prototype topology fixture for historical-claim risk, then route onward to systems_architect and qa_reviewer for authority-boundary and validation review.
